div1.entryFree: Thrall, vb. to enslave: nor falls under the blow of --ed discontent, Sonn. 124, 7. that maid whose sudden sight hath --ed my wounded eye, Shr. I, 1, 225. sense to ecstasy was ne'er so --ed but it reserved some quantity of choice, Hml. III, 4, 74. cf. Enthralled, which is, almost exclusively, used of the servitude of love.
